title: phlox viridis subsp. longipes
intro: Phlox viridis subsp. longipes, also known as green phlox, is a perennial herb belonging to the phlox family that is native to North America. The plant's leaves and stems have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Phlox viridis subsp. longipes include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
